docs.title: Absolutely Plausible — Business Plan
docs.text: Open Source // Cyberpunk // Revolutionary

docs.title: What We Do
docs.text:
Absolutely Plausible is the business umbrella behind everything built at robotfantome.com.

We build tools, systems, and experiences — for ourselves and for clients — with a bias toward open source, sustainability, and things that actually work.

docs.title: Three Revenue Streams
docs.text: Stream What Status Mechanic Work Traveling karting mechanic (PCJ) — primary income Active AI Consulting AI Audits ($200), workflow builds, retainers Building hackFatura Custom builds ($500) for karting mechanics Building Content + Music LinkedIn, YouTube, Gumroad products, robot fantôme EP Building

docs.title: Financial Reality
docs.text: Amount Monthly survival minimum $1,750/mo Mechanic income (PCJ) $750/mo Online income target $1,500/mo PCJ status Stays — online income is additive

docs.title: Current Focus (March 2026)
docs.text:
• Workspace and agent infrastructure set up
  • hackFatura built for PCJ — live at wmestrinho.github.io/hackFatura
  • Brand identity documented
  • Business strategy and 6-month timeline written
  • hackFatura: connect Google Sheets backend end-to-end
  • DNS: point mech.robotfantome.com at GitHub Pages
  • First custom hackFatura build sold ($500)
  • First AI Audit delivered ($200)
  • Gumroad product live ($29)
  • robotfantome.com v1 live

See Roadmap & Checklist for the full weekly tracker.

docs.title: The Plan in One Sentence
docs.text:
Sell custom field management apps to karting mechanics, consult on AI automation for small businesses, and build in public — all under the Robot Fantôme brand, at $0/month infrastructure cost.

See Strategy and Timeline for the full picture.

docs.title: Principles
docs.text:
• $0 infrastructure until revenue justifies spend
  • 100% open-source — OSS tools and solutions always
  • Zero waste / zero pollution — sustainability is a principle, not a preference
  • Build in public — transparency is the brand
  • Charge early — validate with money, not likes
  • 10-15 hours/week — plan for reality, not fantasy

Luiz Wagner Mestrinho — robotfantome.com

docs.title: The Rule
docs.text: Our work (Absolutely Plausible / robotfantome.com) → Robot Fantôme clean editorial palette Client work → client's own palette Check this page before building any UI.

docs.title: Robot Fantôme — Clean Editorial
docs.text:
White space-forward, content-driven, editorial. The art and music provide the color. The frame stays neutral.

Role Hex Usage Background #FFFFFF Primary background Off-White #F5F5F5 Subtle section backgrounds Black #000000 Primary text, headings Dark Gray #333333 Secondary text, body copy Mid Gray #888888 Captions, metadata, subtle UI Light Gray #E2E2E2 Borders, dividers, cards

Typography: Serif headings (Playfair Display or Libre Baskerville), clean sans body (Inter or DM Sans), JetBrains Mono for code.

Keywords: Ghost in the machine, open-source, community, art magazine, zine, manifesto.

Voice: Community-first, punk ethos, DIY, anti-corporate, build your own tools, "absolutely plausible."

docs.title: Client: PCJ / Parazinho Chassis Jig
docs.text:
Industrial, mechanical. Bold, grounded. Used in hackFatura.

Role Hex Background #0A0A0F Primary Red #E74C3C / #C0392B Graphite Surface #333333 Text #F0F0F0

Typography: Orbitron (display), Share Tech Mono (body). Cyberpunk, dark theme, scanlines.

Color Rule

All work for PCJ must use the PCJ palette — never the Robot Fantôme editorial palette.

docs.title: Adding a New Client
docs.text:
Copy this template and fill it in before building any client UI:

## Client: [Business Name]

**Owner:** [Name]
**Project:** [Project name and what it does]
**Relationship:** Built by Absolutely Plausible for [client]

| Role | Hex |
|------|-----|
| Background | `` |
| Primary | `` |
| Text | `` |

**Typography:** [describe]

docs.title: Absolutely Plausible
docs.text:
The business entity. The umbrella. Everything we build that isn't someone else's brand lives here.

  • Domain: robotfantome.com, robotfantome.crypto
  • Focus: Web development, AI tooling, freelance work, developer advocacy, digital products
  • Aesthetic: Clean, minimal, editorial. White space is a feature. See Brand.

docs.title: Luiz Wagner Mestrinho
docs.text:
Traveling mechanic. Freelance developer. AI builder. Punk musician. Classical choral singer.

  • Timezone: America/New_York (EST) | Central Florida (Winter Park area)
  • Values: 100% open-source, zero-waste/zero-pollution, privacy-first
  • Working style: Direct, lean, bias toward action
  • Unfair advantage: Deep karting access + ships real software + AI-native + two music careers (punk AND classical) + Bach Festival Choir network

docs.title: robot fantôme
docs.text:
The punk music project. Indie/Punk/Rock. EP with 5 songs released and live.

  • French for "robot ghost"
  • Tracks infused into ALL Robot Fantôme Ventures content — intros, outros, backgrounds
  • Zero licensing costs, fully owned — a moat no other tech creator has

docs.title: Bach Festival Choir
docs.text:
~10 years as a member of the Bach Festival Society of Winter Park.

  • Audition-only, 150-voice choir + full orchestra. Founded 1935.
  • WSJ: "by any possible standard, one of the finest organizations of its kind in America"
  • PBS appearances. Tours with the London Symphony Orchestra. Italian tour 2025.
  • Repertoire: Bach, Verdi, Mozart, Brahms, Rossini — the canon of choral masterworks
  • Monday evening rehearsals, mid-August through May
  • ~150 musicians in Central Florida = warm professional network

The contrast between choir and punk is not a contradiction — it's the most honest version of who Luiz is. Bach on Monday, punk on Saturday.

docs.title: The Ghost in the Machine
docs.text:
The identity thread that runs through everything: a force that moves fast, fixes things, doesn't leave a trace of waste, and operates in the background until something needs doing.

Open Source. Cyberpunk. Revolutionary.

docs.title: Business Strategy — Robot Fantôme Ventures
docs.text:
"A mechanic who codes, builds AI agents, and plays punk rock walks into a market gap..."

Created: 2026-03-01 | Review cycle: Monthly

docs.title: Business Structure
docs.text:
Robot Fantôme (Absolutely Plausible) is OUR business — the umbrella brand for everything we build, sell, and create.

hackFatura is a CLIENT PROJECT — built for Parazinho Chassis Jig (PCJ), owned by Thiago Pereira. It is NOT our brand.

Robot Fantôme (Us) Client Projects (hackFatura, etc.) Brand Clean, white, editorial, minimalist Client's own Domain robotfantome.com mech.robotfantome.com (subdomain) Revenue Consulting, content, SaaS, products Client deliverable → portfolio + case study Music Infused in OUR content only Not in client deliverables

docs.title: Financial Reality
docs.text: Amount Notes Monthly survival minimum $1,750/mo Rent + food + bills + car payment + supplies Mechanic income $750/mo Current baseline from PCJ Online income needed $1,500/mo Additive income target Runway without mechanic work 0-1 months PCJ stays — online revenue is additive

docs.title: The Unfair Advantage
docs.text:
• Deep karting industry access — works for Thiago Pereira (PCJ), who has 19 years in karting chassis. Embedded in the industry at 80+ events/year, 22+ tracks.
  • Builder — ships real software (hackFatura: zero-cost field app, live in a single session)
  • AI-native — runs own agent infrastructure, understands automation deeply
  • 100% OSS + sustainability values — brand differentiator, not a constraint
  • Original music — robot fantôme tracks in ALL content (unique sonic brand, no licensing costs)

docs.title: Priority Order
docs.text:
Priority Stream Why #1 Consulting (Plan B) Fastest to revenue, highest $/hour #2 hackFatura (Plan A) Product exists, customers in your network #3 Content (Plan C) Force multiplier but slowest to monetize

When time is scarce (travel weeks), work ONLY on #1 and #2.

docs.title: PLAN A: hackFatura — Custom Builds → SaaS
Phase 1 (Month 1-3): Custom Builds
docs.text:
• Custom-branded hackFatura builds for other mechanics
  • Price: $500 per setup (~2-3 hours with AI assistance)
  • Target: mechanics you already know from the karting circuit
  • "Built by Robot Fantôme" footer credit

docs.title: Phase 2 (Month 4-6): SaaS — Only If Demand Proves It
docs.text:
Decision gate: 5+ builds sold AND people asking to sign up online? → Build SaaS. Otherwise keep selling custom builds.

Tier Price Features Starter $19/mo Single user, 100 invoices/mo, basic dashboard Pro $39/mo Multi-user (up to 5), unlimited invoices, custom branding Team $79/mo Unlimited users, multi-location, analytics, API access

docs.title: Revenue Projection
docs.text: Month Custom Builds SaaS MRR Total 1-3 $500-1,000/mo $0 $500-1,000/mo 4-6 on-demand $200-500 $500-1,000/mo

docs.title: Service Ladder
docs.text: Service Price Deliverable AI Audit $200 30-min call + written report Workflow Build $750-2,500 Custom automation (Sheets + Apps Script) Zero-Cost MVP $3,000-7,000 Full app like hackFatura Monthly Retainer $750-1,500/mo Ongoing maintenance + new automations

docs.title: Positioning
docs.text:
Anti-agency. Anti-bloat. Anti-vendor-lock-in.

"I build real business tools for $0/month. No subscriptions. No hosting fees. You own everything."

docs.title: Decision Triggers
docs.text: Signal Action 3+ audits → 0 projects Rework the pitch Demand > capacity Raise prices 25% 2+ retainers by Month 3 Consulting is your core — double down

docs.title: Content Pillars (Pick 2 First)
docs.text:
1. "Zero-Cost Tech" — Build real tools for $0/month
  2. "Mechanic Who Codes" — The personal brand story

docs.title: Realistic Content Volume
docs.text: Channel Frequency Notes LinkedIn 2x/week Batch write on Sundays Newsletter 2x/month Buttondown (free) YouTube 2x/month Record at track when possible

docs.title: Digital Products
docs.text: When Product Price Month 1 "Zero-Cost Business App Blueprint" (Gumroad) $29 Month 3 "Google Apps Script Templates" (5-pack) $39 Month 5-6 Course (only if 500+ newsletter subs) $99-149

docs.title: Music as Content Weapon
docs.text: robot fantôme music in everything: YouTube intros/outros, tutorial backgrounds, product launches. No licensing costs. No copyright strikes. A moat no other tech creator has.

docs.title: The Flywheel
docs.text:
Content + Music (Plan C)
          ↙         ↘
     Credibility    Audience
          ↓            ↓
     Consulting     hackFatura
     (Plan B)       (Plan A)
          ↘         ↙
       Case Studies + Revenue
          ↓
       More Content → repeat

docs.title: Decision Gates
docs.text: When Decision If YES If NO End Month 1 Anyone paid for an audit? Keep going Rework offer End Month 3 5+ hackFatura builds + SaaS demand? Start SaaS dev Keep custom builds End Month 3 Any retainer clients? Consulting is core Diagnose and adjust End Month 5 500+ newsletter subs? Build the course Skip course, keep growing

docs.title: Core Principles (Non-Negotiable)
docs.text:
• $0 infrastructure until revenue justifies spend
  • Open-source first — always
  • Zero-waste stack — minimal cloud footprint
  • Build in public — transparency is the brand
  • Original music everywhere — robot fantôme is the soundtrack
  • PCJ mechanic work stays — online revenue is additive
  • Charge early, charge often
  • 10-15 hours/week — plan for reality, not fantasy

This strategy is alive. Review monthly. Kill what doesn't work. Double down on what does.

docs.title: 6-Month Timeline — Robot Fantôme Ventures
docs.text: Start date: 2026-03-01 North star: $3,000-5,000/mo online income by September 2026 Constraint: PCJ mechanic work continues. All online work fits in 10-15 hours/week.

docs.title: Month 1 — March 2026: First Dollars
docs.text:
"Prove people will pay. Everything else is noise."

Max 5 tasks per week. Revenue tasks first.

docs.title: Week 1 — Validate & Sell
docs.text:
• Fix hackFatura backend (redeploy Code.gs, verify Sheets end-to-end)
  • Contact 3 mechanics about custom hackFatura builds ($500)
  • Contact 3 small business owners about AI Audits ($200)
  • Write + publish first LinkedIn post
  • Record 15-sec intro + 30-sec outro clips (robot fantôme)

docs.title: Week 2 — First Delivery + Digital Product
docs.text:
• Deliver first AI Audit ($200)
  • Create "Zero-Cost Business App Blueprint" on Gumroad ($29)
  • Publish 2 LinkedIn posts
  • Start first custom hackFatura build (if mechanic committed)

docs.title: Week 3 — Website + Booking
docs.text:
• Deploy robotfantome.com v1 (Astro + GitHub Pages)
  • Set up DNS: mech.robotfantome.com
  • 2 LinkedIn posts
  • Follow up all leads

docs.title: Week 4 — Compound + Review
docs.text:
• Deliver builds/audits in pipeline
  • Set up Buttondown newsletter
  • Monthly retrospective: actual revenue vs target

docs.title: March Targets
docs.text: Metric Minimum Stretch Revenue $700 $2,000 AI Audits delivered 2 4 hackFatura builds sold 0 2 LinkedIn posts 6 8 Gumroad product Live 5+ sales robotfantome.com Live With booking

docs.title: Month 2 — April: Build the Machine
docs.text:
"Systems, not sprints."

  • 3-4 audits + 1-2 projects
  • Push for first retainer client ($750-1,500/mo)
  • hackFatura builds on demand ($500 each)
  • LinkedIn 2x/week, newsletter 2x/month
  • First YouTube video, first case study
Metric Minimum Stretch Revenue $1,200 $3,500 First retainer Pitched Signed Newsletter subs 50 100

docs.title: Month 3 — May: Real Recurring Revenue
docs.text:
"Retainers are the game."

  • Target: 2 retainer clients ($1,500-3,000/mo recurring)
  • SaaS Decision Gate: 5+ builds sold + people asking to sign up online?
    • YES → Start SaaS development
    • NO → Keep selling custom builds
  • Second Gumroad product ($39)
  • Guest appearance (podcast, Twitter Space, etc.)
Metric Minimum Stretch Revenue $2,000 $5,000 Consulting MRR $750 $2,000 Newsletter subs 150 250

docs.title: Month 4 — June: Double Down on Winners
docs.text:
"Cut what doesn't work. Pour fuel on what does."

  • Winning stream gets 50% of time
  • 3+ retainer clients — if at capacity, raise prices 25%
  • hackFatura SaaS beta (if gate passed) or continued custom builds
  • Content repurposing: 1 YouTube → LinkedIn → newsletter
Metric Minimum Stretch Revenue $2,500 $6,500 Consulting MRR $1,500 $3,000

docs.title: Month 5 — July: Leverage
docs.text:
"Work ON the business, not IN it."

  • Systemize consulting delivery (reduce per-project time 30%)
  • hackFatura SEO + self-serve onboarding (if SaaS)
  • Course Decision Gate: 500+ newsletter subs?
    • YES → Begin course development
    • NO → Skip course, keep growing audience
Metric Minimum Stretch Revenue $3,000 $7,500 Consulting MRR $2,000 $4,000

docs.title: Month 6 — August: The Flywheel Spins
docs.text:
"Everything feeds everything."

  • Course launch (if built) — target 20-30 sales ($2,000-4,500)
  • 4+ retainer clients
  • Newsletter sponsor deal (if 500+ subs)
  • New single release timed with product launch
  • Portfolio: 4+ case studies on robotfantome.com
Metric Minimum Stretch Revenue $3,500 $12,000 Consulting MRR $2,500 $5,000 hackFatura MRR $300 $800

docs.title: Weekly Rhythm
docs.text:
Day Focus Sunday Plan + review, batch write LinkedIn posts Monday Consulting (audits, projects, calls) Tuesday hackFatura (dev, builds, support) Wednesday Outreach (follow-ups, DMs, networking) Thursday Content (YouTube, newsletter, Gumroad) Friday Consulting (delivery, proposals) Saturday Karting — USE hackFatura, NETWORK, SELL

Travel weeks: Outreach + follow-ups only. Don't try to build.

Revenue from Day 1. That's the difference between a plan and a dream.

docs.title: hackFatura
docs.text: Full field management system built for PCJ / Parazinho Chassis Jig.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Field Value Client PCJ / Parazinho Chassis Jig Owner Thiago Pereira Built by Absolutely Plausible Relationship Luiz works at PCJ as a mechanic; hackFatura is built for the shop Brand PCJ palette — black/red/graphite. See Brand. Live URL github.com/wmestrinho/hackFatura App URL wmestrinho.github.io/hackFatura Subdomain mech.robotfantome.com (DNS pending)

docs.title: What It Does
docs.text:
Full trackside field management covering:

  • New Customer registration
  • Invoice Builder — jsPDF client-side PDF generation, PCJ branded, works offline
  • Table Work — event-based service logging (alignment, tuning, etc.)
  • Parts Work — parts inventory and tracking per kart
  • Work Costs — expense tracking per event
  • Dashboard — event summary: revenue, costs, invoices pending

docs.title: Stack
docs.text:
Layer Tech Frontend Static HTML/CSS/JS — modular (4 JS files) Backend Google Apps Script (free, deploy as Web App) Database Google Sheets (free, auto-creates tabs + headers) PDF generation jsPDF — fully client-side, offline-capable Hosting GitHub Pages via GitHub Actions CI/CD Custom domain mech.robotfantome.com (CNAME pending)

Zero monthly cost. No n8n. No paid services. No server.

docs.title: Team (5 users)
docs.text: Wagner (Dev/Owner) · Thiago (Manager) · Bebeco (Dad) · Samuel (Supervisor) · Henrique (Mechanic)

docs.title: Status
docs.text:
• Full app built — all 6 modules
  • Google Apps Script backend (GET-based, CORS-safe)
  • jsPDF invoice generator — PCJ branded, offline-capable
  • GitHub Actions CI/CD — auto-deploys on every push (~30s)
  • Live at wmestrinho.github.io/hackFatura
  • Apps Script deployed with correct Spreadsheet ID
  • Google Sheets connection verified end-to-end
  • DNS: CNAME mech → wmestrinho.github.io at robotfantome.com DNS
  • GitHub Pages custom domain → mech.robotfantome.com

docs.title: Files
docs.text:
projects/hackFatura/
├── index.html                       # Full app, all 6 modules
├── css/style.css                    # Cyberpunk theme, scanlines, glows
├── js/
│   ├── config.js                    # Pricing, team, business info (single source of truth)
│   ├── sheets.js                    # Google Apps Script API layer
│   ├── invoice.js                   # jsPDF invoice generator
│   └── app.js                       # All logic, forms, kart tags, events, dashboard
├── google-apps-script/
│   └── Code.gs                      # Backend: 6 actions, all sheet tabs
├── .github/workflows/deploy.yml     # GitHub Pages CI/CD
└── assets/                          # Logos, branding

docs.title: Business Logic (Pricing)
docs.text: Service Price Fine Tuning (1st visit, <5mm) $150 Significant (1st visit, >5mm / multi-area) $180 2nd visit $100 3rd visit $50 Weekend Package $350 (regular customer: $300) Salvage — 1 area $150 → 2: $180 → 3: $210 → 4: $250 → 5+: $300 Spindle $40 Column/Shaft $30 Axle 30mm / 40mm / 50mm $40 / $50 / $60 Chassis inspection FREE

docs.title: 2026-03-01 — Strategy v2 Improvements
docs.text:
• Financial reality check added ($1,750/mo survival, $750/mo PCJ, $1,500/mo online target)
  • Fixed experience attribution (19 years is Thiago/PCJ, not Luiz)
  • Raised prices: AI Audit $200, retainers $750-1,500, SaaS $19/$39/$79
  • Added decision gates (if/then triggers for SaaS, course, price raises)
  • Reduced content volume to realistic 10-15 hrs/week capacity
  • Delayed SaaS to Month 4+ (custom builds validate first)
  • Priority ranking: Consulting #1, hackFatura #2, Content #3
  • Added travel week protocol: outreach + follow-ups only
